name: on frSymbol
title: on frSymbol
description: The event procedure is called whenever a symbol (wake-up, CAS, MTS) is received.
return:
The symbol time stamp that has been synchronized with the global time 
 base in the

description: The following program reacts on received symbols and prints them in the Write Window, Trace Window, and logging.

availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
CANoe Test Setup for Test Units

row: The symbol time stamp that has been synchronized with the global time base in the computer (CAN hardware or computer system clock). The time stamp must be used if time relations should be regarded with events from other sources. This time stamp is also output in the Trace Window when receiving a symbol.. Unit:10 microseconds Write-protected!
Channel in the tool that the FlexRay CC determines. Write-protected!
Identifies the FlexRay channel of the CC. With 1 the symbol was received on channel A, with 2 on channel B. Write-protected!

Identifies the symbol type (data type: WORD) 0 unknown 1 CAS 2 MTS 3 Wakeup 4 Undefined (used with VN interfaces in asynchronous mode) Caution! Any wake-up symbol will only be received, if the wake-up was initiated externally. Thus, if CANoe initiates a wake-up procedure, then use the handler on frPocState in order to observe the wake-up pattern transmission! Write-protected!
Undefined (used with VN interfaces in asynchronous mode)

Caution! Any wake-up symbol will only be received, if the wake-up was initiated externally. Thus, if CANoe initiates a wake-up procedure, then use the handler on frPocState in order to observe the wake-up pattern transmission!
Undefined (used with VN interfaces in asynchronous mode)

Caution! Any wake-up symbol will only be received, if the wake-up was initiated externally. Thus, if CANoe initiates a wake-up procedure, then use the handler on frPocState in order to observe the wake-up pattern transmission!
Indicates the length (data type: WORD) of the symbol in bit times. Write-protected!
